title: Agent Action
description: The strict JSON object an agent's model must return each turn. Parsed from the model's text output (first valid JSON object). On parse/validation failure: one retry with the error appended; second failure -> idle. v1.1.0 (W5 / EM-066) adds OPTIONAL decision-trace fields (perceived_summary, memories_used, reasoning) captured in the SAME single call -> zero extra LLM calls. v1.2.0 (EM-199, multi-action turns) adds an OPTIONAL `actions` array: an ORDERED sequence of up to max_actions_per_turn steps applied in order, all from this single call, emitted as one _multi chain sharing one turn_id (see contracts/multi-action-turns.md). The single `action` form stays valid byte-for-byte. Turn-level cognition fields (thought, mood, perceived_summary, memories_used, reasoning) stay singular. The optional natural-language trace fields are TRUNCATED to their maxLength by the engine before validation; only structural args (action, target, place, amount) are validated strictly, and for the `actions[]` form per-step args are validated at resolution (continue-on-failure). See contracts/event-log.md.

thought.description: Brief private reasoning. Not broadcast. Turn-level (one per turn even with multiple actions).

perceived_summary.description: OPTIONAL (EM-066). One sentence on what the agent perceived this turn (who/what was nearby, what was overheard). Recorded into the `perceived` event-log row.

memories_used.description: OPTIONAL (EM-066). The memory/observation snippets (or their ids) the agent actually leaned on. Recorded into the `memory_retrieved` / `reasoning` rows.

reasoning.description: OPTIONAL (EM-066). Fuller chain of thought leading to this action. Recorded into the `reasoning` event-log row; distinct from `thought` (a short private blurb).

action.description: A SINGLE action (legacy / minimal form). Provide this OR `actions` (a sequence). If both are present, `actions` wins.

actions.description: EM-199 multi-action turn: an ORDERED sequence of steps applied in order, all from this single LLM call, sharing one turn_id. PREFERRED over a single `action` when doing several things (e.g. move somewhere, act there, then say something about it). Steps resolve independently — each goes through the SAME per-step _normalize_args (arg-alias + name resolution) and _validate_world gate as a single action, and a gated/failed step does NOT abort the rest. The REAL cap is the runtime `max_actions_per_turn` config (default 4); extra steps are dropped (logged). maxItems here is a generous schema ceiling, not the operative limit.

items.comment: additionalProperties TRUE: the runtime reads only action+args; models often nest per-action cognition (thought/perceived_summary/memories_used/reasoning) into a step, and a misplaced cosmetic field must never fail the turn (EM-066). Stray cognition on the first step is hoisted to top-level before validation.

examples.thought: I'm broke and hungry, time to earn.
Ada keeps stealing. Propose a ban.

args.text: Anyone want to team up at the market?

examples.perceived_summary: Bram is beside me in the plaza and looks low on energy; Cleo just proposed a UBI rule.
memories_used: Bram gave me 3 credits last round
the market pays best for work
examples.reasoning: Bram helped me before and is struggling, so repaying builds an ally and trust; I can spare 3 credits.

examples.thought: Fund the well, then rally the plaza.

args.text: I chipped in for the well — who's with me?
